channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 12 U.S. Troops Injured in Attack on Saudi Base, Officials Say
item.description: Two of the Americans were seriously wounded in the strike on the Prince Sultan Air Base, the officials said, in one of the most serious breaches of American defenses since the war began on Feb. 28.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: UAE and Qatar Arrest Hundreds Over Online Videos of Iranian Attacks
item.description: The authorities have made a wave of arrests to stop people posting footage of strikes, citing security risks. Experts also see a fear of damage to the countries’ image as safe havens.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Inspecting damage this month from a drone strike at a hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Nepal’s New Prime Minister, Former Rapper Balendra Shah, Sworn In
item.description: A leaked report on the deadly violence and mass arson that broke out last year has put the new leader under pressure to ensure accountability.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Binod Ghimire
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah, right, with Rabi Lamichhane, the founder of his party, campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: U.S. May Label Brazilian Gangs as Terror Groups, After Push by the Bolsonaros
item.description: The Trump administration is weighing declaring Brazil’s two largest drug gangs terrorist groups, after lobbying by the sons of jailed former President Jair Bolsonaro.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Edward Wong
item.media:credit: Dado Galdieri for The New York Times
item.media:description: Drug traffickers, part of an organized criminal group, gathered after a police operation in a favela in 2019.

item.title: In Sleepy Town on Strait of Hormuz, War Rages Just Over Horizon
item.description: For centuries, an Omani exclave has been defined by a peculiar duality: rugged isolation and proximity to one of the world’s most important trade routes.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.N. Report Documents Sectarian Violence in Syria That Killed Over 1,700
item.description: Investigators said the government of President Ahmed al-Sharaa had done little to address the extrajudicial killings and torture of Druse civilians in July 2025.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bodies recovered from Syrian villages were taken to Sweida National Hospital in Syria for identification last year.

item.title: Noelia Castillo Ramos Dies in Spain After Winning Right to End Her Life
item.description: Noelia Castillo Ramos, 25, who was in chronic physical and psychological pain, had sought an assisted death since 2024, but her father sued to prevent it.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Carlos Barragán
item.media:credit: Lorena Sopena/Europa Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Outside the hospital north of Barcelona where Noelia Castillo Ramos died on Thursday. Her case divided Spain, where medically assisted death has been legal since 2021.

item.title: The German Military Tightens Its Social Media Rules
item.description: Military members sharing clips on Instagram and TikTok have helped recruit badly needed new soldiers, but Bundeswehr officials said they are concerned about security.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Tatiana Firsova
item.media:description: Bundeswehr recruits at a training in Ahlen, Germany, in November.

item.title: Actress Says She’s Found Her Secret Online Abuser: Her Husband
item.description: Collien Fernandes said her spouse had posted deepfakes of her. The account, disputed by her husband, spurred outrage, protests and parliamentary debates in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:description: The actress Collien Fernandes speaking on Thursday in Hamburg, Germany, at a demonstration protesting online abuse of women.

item.title: Europeans Are Angry at Trump, but Often Forgiving of Americans
item.description: A generation ago, foreign fury over the Iraq invasion often blurred into anti-Americanism. Now, some Europeans seem ready to distinguish between the president and the American people.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Washington this month.

item.title: The Faroe Islands, Wary After Greenland, Vote for Change
item.description: Statehood had been a key issue in this tiny Danish archipelago before President Trump threatened Greenland. Now, Faroese voters are focused more on their own economy than geopolitics.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Regin Winther Poulsen
item.media:credit: Alvur Haraldsen/Ritzau Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Counting votes in Torshavn, the capital of the Faroe Islands, on Thursday.

item.title: Lee Geun-an, Infamous ‘Torture Master’ Under South Korean Dictator, Dies at 88
item.description: The name of Mr. Lee, a former police inspector, had long incited fear and hatred in the country.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Lee Geun-an during a launch event for his autobiography in 2012 in Seoul.

item.title: Cuban Patients Are Dying Because of U.S. Blockade, Doctors Say
item.description: Cuban health care was once the pride of the island. Now the U.S. oil blockade is upending even basic medical care.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin, Jack Nicas and Jorge Luis Baños
item.media:credit: Jorge Luis Baños for The New York Times

item.title: Olympic Committee Bars Transgender Athletes From Women’s Events
item.description: The decision is the most significant since Kirsty Coventry was elected last year to serve as president of the I.O.C.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Kirsty Coventry, president of the International Olympic Committee and a former Olympic swimmer from Zimbabwe.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Chris Cameron and John Yoon

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: American officials sought to temper concerns of a possibly expanding military incursion after the Pentagon’s decision to deploy more troops to the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israel on Friday launched airstrikes on Dahiya, an area on the outskirts of Lebanon’s capital that is a Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: Strikes on Iranian Industry Expand Blows to Civilian Economy
item.description: After Israel said it would intensify attacks on Iran’s industrial infrastructure, major steel plants and other vital plants were struck.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan, Iran, in 2012. The governor of Isfahan said one person was killed and 15 were injured when Israel attacked the complex on Friday.

item.title: Iran Moves to Assert Control Over Strait While Trading Strikes With Israel
item.description: Two ships turned back after being warned not to pass through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Iran said strikes in the country had hit a uranium processing plant and industrial sites.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cleaning up rubble in an apartment damaged by airstrikes in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Government Trolls Sling Memes in the Online Trenches of Mideast War
item.description: Officials in Tehran and Washington alike are trading taunts in English, often using American pop culture references.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran this month showing Iran’s three supreme leaders since the Islamic Revolution.

item.title: Europe Is Drafting Postwar Plan to Escort Tankers, Officials Say
item.description: France and Britain are leading planning to have warships escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, which officials say is farther along than has been revealed publicly.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in Muscat, Oman, earlier this month, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed to shipping traffic.

item.title: Elon Musk Joins in Phone Call Between Donald Trump and Narendra Modi
item.description: Mr. Musk’s participation was notable because it is rare for a private citizen to be on a call between heads of state. It also suggests that Mr. Musk is back on better terms with the president.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Theodore Schleifer, Tyler Pager and Ryan Mac
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Shivon Zilis arriving for a wedding at Mar-a-Lago last month.

item.title: Iran Tried to Use a Famed Director’s Home to Push Its War Narrative. His Son Fired Back.
item.description: The Tehran home of the acclaimed Iranian director Abbas Kiarostami was damaged in strikes this week. His son Ahmad pushed back on efforts by the government to seize on his legacy.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The filmmaker Abbas Kiarostami at a Film Festival in Dubai in 2012.

item.title: Iran Moves to Formalize Toll Plan in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran has effectively closed off the critical waterway, turning back container ships on Friday, and Iranian lawmakers are considering whether to formalize charging fees to pass.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran has effectively cut off traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, leaving thousands of vessels idling on either side of it.

item.title: Global Food Supply Faces a Dangerous Bottleneck as Iran War Persists
item.description: Fertilizer prices are climbing as a result of disruptions in the Middle East, putting global food supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A farmer spread fertilizer on his mustard field this week in Pampore, India. Large parts of Asia, particularly India and Thailand, are most exposed to fertilizer supply shortages.

item.title: Did Scientists Just Detect an Exploding Black Hole?
item.description: An underwater observatory recently detected a startlingly energetic cosmic neutrino. One possible cause involves a phenomenon that so far exists only in theory.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Overbye

item.title: Energy Crisis Forces India’s Eateries to Adapt
item.description: A small street stall and a big restaurant chain in India rethought how to cook after the war in the Middle East squeezed their supply of liquified petroleum gas.
item.dc:creator: Nailah Morgan, Karan Deep Singh and Jon Hazell

item.title: Soaring Diesel Prices Set Off Transport Strike in Philippines
item.description: Transport workers in Manila walked off the job to protest the rising cost of fuel, as the war in the Middle East causes a national energy emergency.
item.dc:creator: Jason Gutierrez and John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Transport workers and activists marching on Friday in Manila in a strike over surging fuel costs.

item.title: Ukraine signed a defense cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Zelensky says.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:credit: Saudi Press Agency, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A photo released by the Saudi government showing President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine meeting with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman on Friday.

item.title: Video: Stranded Humpback Whale Beats the Odds and Swims Out to Sea
item.description: In an unexpected breakthrough, the 40-foot mammal was freed and swam to deeper water on Friday morning through a channel that rescuers had dug for it.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.media:description: A humpback whale in the Baltic Sea on Friday. It had been stuck in shallow water near the coast of Germany for four days.

item.title: U.K. Police to Reinvestigate Sex Crime Allegations Against Andrew Tate
item.description: A police force in England said it would look again at allegations against the influencer from 11 years ago. Mr. Tate was not charged in the case and denies any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: A police force in England said it was reinvestigating sex crime allegations against Andrew Tate.

item.title: Rubio Is Expected to Press G7 Allies Over Strait of Hormuz During Meeting
item.description: The meeting in France is slated to discuss efforts to stop the war in the Middle East, end Iran’s nuclear and ballistic missile development, and reopen maritime trade routes.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arriving at an airport in Le Bourget, France, on Friday for a Group of 7 foreign ministers’ meeting.

item.title: Resurgent Inflation Tests Faith in Fed’s Willingness to Tame It
item.description: The war in the Middle East risks worsening an inflation problem that the Federal Reserve has struggled for years to subdue.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith and Ben Casselman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Progress on lowering inflation had stalled even before the war with Iran, as tariffs caused higher prices on imported goods, and solid consumer spending led to increased cost for services.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Chris Cameron, Farnaz Fassihi, Shirin Hakim and Mark Landler

item.title: Italy Says It Recovered $23 Million Stolen From Ursula Andress, First ‘Bond Girl’
item.description: The Swiss actress Ursula Andress, whose breakthrough came in “Dr. No” in 1962, accused a former wealth manager of embezzlement. He died in an apparent suicide last year.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:description: Ursula Andress in 2012. She is best known for her role in the first James Bond film.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: For a second time, President Trump extended the deadline for Iran to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: A bedroom in ruins after a missile attack in Kafr Qasim, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: As Markets Revolt in the Face of War, Trump Extends Iran Deadline
item.description: Just after stocks ended another bruising day, the president took to social media to promote progress in talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Traders working on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange in New York City, on Wednesday.

item.title: Rubio Says Allies Should Help Secure Strait by Iran for Oil and Gas Ships
item.description: The secretary of state said the United States and Iran were passing messages to each other as he headed to France for a diplomatic meeting of the Group of 7 nations.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, speaking at a cabinet meeting at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Displaced by War, Many Seek Shelter in Beirut
item.description: Over 100,000 people have fled to temporary shelters as Israel continues strikes on Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaito, Anushka Patil and Diego Ibarra Sanchez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Zouheir Chahine fled his hometown in southern Lebanon and now lives at a temporary shelter at a local school in the capital, Beirut.

item.title: Betting on Everything
item.description: Prediction market platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi go way beyond sports and politics. We take a closer look.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times

item.title: Standoff With Iran Raises Fresh Doubts About Trump’s Freestyle Diplomacy
item.description: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump’s improvisational approach.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: As the war stretches on, President Trump appears to be casting about for a diplomatic offramp even as he threatens an escalation.

item.title: Pakistan, Playing Mediator Between U.S. and Iran, Is Calling Several Countries
item.description: Washington, Tehran, Riyadh, Abu Dhabi, Cairo, Istanbul and Brussels have all received calls as Pakistan plays mediator between the United States and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Salman Masood
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan.

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Deadline on Strait of Hormuz as Stocks Tumble
item.description: President Trump pivoted after escalating threats. Israel also announced it had killed an Iranian commander leading efforts to block the Strait of Hormuz to almost all shipping traffic.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Adam Rasgon, Eshe Nelson and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio, left, and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, on Thursday at the White House. Mr. Trump said if Iran did not negotiate, “we’ll just keep blowing them away.”

item.title: Air Canada C.E.O. Apologizes for English-Language Condolences After Plane Crash
item.description: Michael Rousseau said he was “deeply saddened” that his ​inability to speak French had diverted attention from the families’ grief.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Nick Lachance/Toronto Star via Getty Images
item.media:description: Air Canada’s chief executive, Michael Rousseau, in 2024. He said he was “still unable” to express himself “adequately in French,” despite his many lessons.

item.title: Winter Sea Ice in the Arctic Ties a Record Low
item.description: Ice plays a vital role in reflecting away planet-warming sunlight. The Arctic is warming much faster than most other parts of the world.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Harry Stevens
item.category: National Snow and Ice Data Center
item.media:description: The disappearing ice also affects wildlife habitat. A polar bear on Norwegian sea ice last April.

item.title: Hungary Opens Espionage Case Against Journalist
item.description: The government of Prime Minister Viktor Orban has pursued an intensifying campaign against critics in a tough race before April elections.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:description: The investigative journalist Szabolcs Panyi in Budapest in 2021. He dismissed the government’s espionage claims as a “false” and “completely unjust accusation.”

item.title: Malaysia Says Iran Will Allow Its Ships to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The reprieve would ease disruptions in Malaysia’s energy supply, but the prime minister has vowed to make preparations for a more volatile future.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships anchored in Muscat, Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz, on Wednesday.

item.title: Scientists Filmed a Whale Birth. The Surprise: Mom Had Many Helpers.
item.description: The episode, involving a group of sperm whales, adds to evidence that humans aren’t the only species that gets some form of assistance during and after delivery.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn

item.title: Europeans Worry Russia Is Preparing to Deliver Drones to Iran
item.description: The drones are an improved version of a weapon that Iran sent to Russia for use in its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ukrainian war crimes prosecutors with a Russian Shahed-style drone, in the Kharkiv region of Ukraine last year. While Iran initially supplied hundreds of drones in 2022, Russia then built its own production facility and evolved the design.

item.title: Under Carney, Canada Finally Hits NATO’s 2% Spending Target
item.description: Trump and other American presidents have criticized Canada for failing to meet the alliance’s military spending minimum of 2 percent of gross domestic product.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Renaud Philippe for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of the Canadian military during an exercise last month on Victoria Island in the country’s Arctic region.

item.title: After Wooing Trump With Deals, Pakistan Gets a Seat at the Table
item.description: Steve Witkoff, a diplomatic envoy, used the Board of Peace to announce an agreement that could raze a Pakistan-owned Manhattan hotel. Now the country is involved in negotiating peace talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany, Ben Protess and Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Roosevelt Hotel in New York, which is owned by Pakistan, in 2023.

item.title: Russian Lawmakers Go to U.S. for First Time Since Invasion of Ukraine
item.description: The State Department had to lift sanctions on Russian lawmakers invited by a Kremlin-friendly member of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:description: Svetlana Zhurova, a member of Russia’s State Duma and a former Olympian, in Saint Petersburg, Russia, in 2022. She is one of four Russian lawmakers traveling to the United States, despite personal sanctions.

item.title: The head of NATO says Europe needs time to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.media:description: NATO’s secretary general, Mark Rutte, said European leaders could not be expected to quickly assist in the Persian Gulf because they were not given advance notice of the Feb. 28 attack on Iran.

item.title: Australia Temporarily Blocks Iranians With Visitor Visas From Entry
item.description: Iranian tourists with visas will be barred for six months in case they are ‘unable or unlikely’ to go back because of the war, Australian officials said.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s embassy in Canberra, Australia.

item.title: Zelensky Says U.S. Is Conditioning Ukraine’s Security Guarantees on Donbas Surrender
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said that President Trump “still chooses a strategy of putting more pressure on the Ukrainian side.”
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky at a command post near Sloviansk, in the eastern Donbas region of Ukraine.

item.title: Two Killed in Abu Dhabi as Gulf States Face Fresh Attacks
item.description: A Pakistani man and an Indian man died when shrapnel fell from an intercepted missile, officials said. Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and Bahrain also reported strikes.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The waterfront in Abu Dhabi in the United Arab Emirates last week.

item.title: U.S. Military Kills 4 People in Boat Strike in Caribbean
item.description: At least 163 people have been killed in the Trump administration’s campaign against suspected drug smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Pentagon said it blew up a boat in the Caribbean on Wednesday, the 47th such attack since the campaign started in early September.

item.title: After ‘Sausage Making,’ European Lawmakers Approve U.S. Trade Deal
item.description: The agreement that President Trump struck with the European Union has cleared a major hurdle that delayed it for months.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Ursula von der Leyen, the president of the European Commission, at the Trump Turnberry golf course in Scotland, where a U.S.-E.U. trade deal was signed last July.

item.title: Who Is Alireza Tangsiri, the Latest Iranian Commander Targeted by Israel?
item.description: Israel said it killed the naval commander, Alireza Tangsiri, in an airstrike on Thursday morning. Iran has not commented.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sepahnews, via Zuma Press Wire, via Reuters
item.media:description: Alireza Tangsiri in a photo released by the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps last month.

item.title: In Rural Ukraine, Basic Health Care Is a Casualty of War
item.description: Elderly people in isolated villages are going without medicine. One woman said she hadn’t seen a doctor in four years.
item.dc:creator: Finbarr O’Reilly
item.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times

item.title: India Appears Sidelined as Pakistan Tries to Play Peacemaker in Iran
item.description: Hours before a call between President Trump and India’s prime minister, American officials urged India to focus on shared goals and ignore differences.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India has endured rocky relations with the United States during President Trump’s second term.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Edward Wong
